mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations when RSAFS is in its top quintile, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% reflects a restrictive monetary stance that raises corporate financing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the Rank IC is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated unemployment and a high federal funds rate indicate a late-cycle stagnation where rising labor slack meets restrictive credit, eroding corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of joint trigger periods, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth during early recovery phases signals accelerating aggregate demand that outpaces cost pressures, lifting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of early-recovery quarters despite positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening goods demand, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1% from prior month
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months following a >1% INDPRO decline, or Rank IC is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate produces negative real interest rates that distort capital allocation and squeeze profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55% of joint trigger periods, or the Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate reflects weakening labor demand that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase, or the single-factor Rank IC is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units signal strong residential investment and construction employment, driving downstream demand for materials and consumer durables and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of weeks when HOUST exceeds 1500, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.
